methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Chai Discovery
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series B), post-money $3.8B

private.name: Eikon Therapeutics

data.narrative:
Basecamp Research, a Series B biotech company founded by Oliver Vince and Glen Gowers, has raised $85M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health, including same-stage comparables Chai Discovery ($3.8B) and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M), as well as later-stage peers Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B) and New Limit ($3.1B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $85M raised proxy, yielding $162M. Peer-median analysis across the four sampled private comparables produces a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range spanning $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target. Notably, the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion: the Series B peer median ($3.5B) substantially exceeds the stage-progression result ($162M), reflecting the wide variance in capital efficiency and exit expectations within the sector.

The triangulated range is $137M (low) to $4.0B (high) with a median of $1.8B. The wide spread reflects divergent market conditions for biotech exits and heterogeneous peer performance. Key uncertainties include Basecamp's clinical-stage progress, intellectual property portfolio depth, and management track record relative to peers. Stage-progression alone underweights peer-market signals; weighting toward the peer-median range may be appropriate if Basecamp demonstrates comparable technical or commercial traction to the sampled cohort.
